target.title: feat(feishu): include reply context in comment auto replies
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Feishu document comment auto replies did not carry source-reply context into the Drive comment write requests, so the receiving side could not distinguish which original comment reply triggered an automatic follow-up.
- Why it matters: comment auto replies need to remain associated with the triggering reply, including whole-document comments where the visible follow-up is created through the add-comment compatibility path instead of the normal reply-comment path.
- What changed: comment-event dispatch, comment-thread outbound delivery, `reply_comment`, and whole-document `add_comment` compatibility now pass a private auto-reply context into Feishu Drive comment writes. The OpenAPI body now includes `extra` with `refer_reply_id` and `ai_reply_source_type` only for event-triggered comment auto replies.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): normal Feishu IM handling was not changed; user-invoked `feishu_drive` comment tools do not add this `extra`; this PR only affects Feishu document comment auto-reply delivery paths.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts

## Root Cause / Regression History

- Root cause: N/A; this is a new Feishu comment auto-reply context field required by the receiving comment write path.
- Missing detection / guardrail: existing tests covered comment delivery but did not assert event-triggered context propagation into the final Drive OpenAPI request body.
- Why this changed now: Feishu document-comment auto replies now need to include their triggering reply context for downstream handling while keeping manual comment tool calls unchanged.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Seam / integration test
- Target test or file: `extensions/feishu/src/comment-dispatcher.test.ts`, `extensions/feishu/src/outbound.test.ts`, `extensions/feishu/src/drive.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: comment-event dispatch and comment-thread outbound delivery must attach auto-reply context; Drive `reply_comment` and whole-document `add_comment` compatibility must serialize `extra`; direct/manual comment writes must remain unchanged unless the ambient comment auto-reply context is present.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Feishu document comment auto replies now preserve the triggering reply context in the comment write payload.
- Whole-document comment auto replies carry the same context when they create a new whole-document comment through the compatibility path.
- Large Feishu reply IDs are preserved as JSON numeric text inside the `extra` string instead of being converted through JS `Number`.
- Normal Feishu IM behavior is unchanged.
- Manual user requests to add or reply to comments through `feishu_drive` are unchanged unless they are running in the auto-reply comment context.

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? (No)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (No)
- New/changed network calls? (No new endpoints; existing Feishu Drive comment write bodies include one additional `extra` field only for auto replies)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (No)
- Data access scope changed? (No)

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (Yes)
- Config/env changes? (No)
- Migration needed? (No)

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: adding `extra` to existing Feishu Drive comment write bodies could be rejected if sent broadly.
  - Mitigation: `extra` is only generated from auto-reply comment context and is not added to normal manual Drive comment tool calls.
- Risk: Feishu reply IDs exceed JS safe integer precision.
  - Mitigation: numeric reply IDs are kept as strings in OpenClaw state and serialized directly into the `extra` JSON string as numeric text without `Number(...)` conversion.
- Risk: whole-document comments use the add-comment compatibility path rather than the normal reply-comment OpenAPI.
  - Mitigation: the same auto-reply context is passed through `deliverCommentThreadText` into both normal reply writes and whole-document add-comment fallback writes, with tests for both paths.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu/src/drive.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/comment-dispatcher.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/outbound.test.ts` - 3 files passed, 60 tests passed.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` - clean, no accepted/actionable findings.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Feishu document comment auto replies now include source reply context on the actual Drive comment write request, including the whole-document add-comment compatibility path.
Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout using the Feishu channel against a real Feishu document-comment auto-reply flow, after building and restarting the local gateway on May 27, 2026.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: In a real Feishu document, created a comment that triggered OpenClaw's Feishu comment auto-reply path, let the local gateway process the event, and observed the resulting Feishu comment reply in the document.
Evidence after fix: Redacted live output from the Feishu comment auto-reply run: the OpenAPI request body carried `extra` as `{"refer_reply_id":7644471294523673786,"ai_reply_source_type":2}`; Feishu accepted the request and created the automatic comment reply.
Observed result after fix: The real Feishu document showed the automatic reply, and the source reply context was preserved in the accepted Drive comment write payload.
What was not tested: Normal Feishu IM behavior was not re-tested because this PR does not change IM handling. No screenshots or recordings are attached; the proof is the redacted copied live output from the local Feishu verification run.

target.title: Add Telegram reliability and handoff commands
target.body:
## Summary
- add `/handoff`, `/resume latest`, and related handoff commands for explicit session handoff text
- add lightweight Telegram inflight state tracking for dispatched/completed/failed/interrupted requests
- send explicit user-facing failure notices for timeout/provider/context/session-lock/delivery failures
- guard very long Telegram inputs when the current session is already high-context
- mark stale inflight requests as interrupted on Telegram runtime startup and notify the original chat without auto-replay
- document the command surface, changelog entry, and conservative rollout/rollback runbook

## Safety boundaries
- Draft PR only; do not deploy directly to production
- does not restart the gateway
- does not retry or replay interrupted requests
- does not cancel or clean tasks
- does not change models, bindings, config, secrets, or sessions
- does not call tasks audit/show from Telegram hot paths
- `/new` alone still starts fresh; handoff is opt-in via `/handoff` then `/resume latest`

## Tests
- `git diff --check`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply-reply.config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-handoff.test.ts`

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Telegram operator messages can currently appear to disappear when gateway/model/session/delivery work is interrupted, and long Telegram sessions require manual handoff text. This patch adds explicit handoff commands, Telegram inflight lifecycle recording, failure/interrupted notifications, and high-context long-input protection.
- **Real environment tested**: Shadow OpenClaw source install on Windows, isolated from production with `--profile codex-shadow-telegram-reliability-token`, token auth, and non-production loopback port `19790`. The shadow profile wrote state under `C:\Users\lcxhh\.openclaw-codex-shadow-telegram-reliability-token`; production OpenClaw config, secrets, sessions, bindings, models, and gateway service were not replaced or restarted.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  - `corepack pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
  - `corepack pnpm build`
  - `node openclaw.mjs --version`
  - `node openclaw.mjs --profile codex-shadow-telegram-reliability-token gateway run --dev --port 19790 --auth token --token [shadow-token] --bind loopback --ws-log compact`
  - `node openclaw.mjs --profile codex-shadow-telegram-reliability-token gateway probe --url ws://127.0.0.1:19790 --timeout 10000 --token [shadow-token] --json`

  - `node openclaw.mjs --profile codex-shadow-telegram-reliability-token gateway status --url ws://127.0.0.1:19790 --timeout 10000 --token [shadow-token] --json`
- **Evidence after fix**: `node openclaw.mjs --version` reported `OpenClaw 2026.5.4 (6c138ab)`. The shadow gateway logged `http server listening` and `ready` on port `19790`. The second shadow `probe` returned `ok: true`, `degraded: false`, `capability: read_only`, `connect.ok: true`, `rpcOk: true`, and `scopes: [operator.read]`. `health` returned `ok: true`; `status` returned `rpc.ok: true` with `url: ws://127.0.0.1:19790`. The shadow process was then stopped and did not remain running.
- **Observed result after fix**: The patched source builds and can start a separate, read-probeable Gateway from a shadow profile without touching production. Targeted tests validate the new handoff, dispatch, inflight, and failure-notice paths: handoff command tests 8/8 passed; Telegram dispatch/bot tests 194/194 passed.
- **What was not tested**: No production deployment, no global package replacement, no production gateway restart, no live Telegram bot token/delivery replacement, no task cleanup, no model/binding/config/secrets/session changes. Live Telegram delivery proof is still intentionally deferred until maintainer review approves a rollout window.

target.title: fix(msteams): pass threadActivityId to attachment sends for threaded replies
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** MS Teams attachment sends (FileConsentCard, SharePoint file cards, OneDrive links) bypassed the threading logic by calling `sendProactiveActivityRaw` without `threadActivityId`. This caused reply messages with attachments to arrive as new top-level messages instead of in-thread replies when `replyStyle: "thread"`.
- **Fix:** Derive `threadActivityId` from `ref.threadId` (or `ref.activityId` as fallback) when `replyStyle === "thread"` and the conversation is a channel or group. Pass it to all three attachment send paths.
- **Out of scope:** No changes to text-only message threading (already works correctly). No changes to the `sendMSTeamsMessages` function.

## Linked context

Closes #88836

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Reply messages with attachments arrive as top-level messages instead of in-thread replies when `channels.msteams.replyStyle = thread`.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux (NewStartOS V4.4.2-ZTE), Node.js v24.13.1, OpenClaw latest main (commit e0e0d283), local checkout with the patch applied.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran `node proof-93837.mjs` which validates the threadActivityId derivation logic for threaded attachment sends.
- **Evidence after fix:**
```
$ node proof-93837.mjs

=== PR #93837: MS Teams ThreadActivityId Proof ===

Testing threadActivityId derivation logic from extensions/msteams/src/send.ts

--- Thread reply in channel conversation ---
Reply style: thread
Conversation type: channel
Thread ID: thread-root-1
Activity ID: activity-1
Expected: thread-root-1
✅ Correct: threadActivityId = thread-root-1

--- Thread reply in groupChat conversation ---
Reply style: thread
Conversation type: groupChat
Thread ID: thread-root-2
Activity ID: activity-2
Expected: thread-root-2
✅ Correct: threadActivityId = thread-root-2

--- Thread reply in personal conversation (no threading) ---
Reply style: thread
Conversation type: personal
Thread ID: thread-root-3
Activity ID: activity-3
Expected: undefined
✅ Correct: threadActivityId = undefined

--- Top-level reply in channel conversation ---
Reply style: top-level
Conversation type: channel
Thread ID: thread-root-4
Activity ID: activity-4
Expected: undefined
✅ Correct: threadActivityId = undefined

--- Thread reply with null threadId (fallback to activityId) ---
Reply style: thread
Conversation type: channel
Thread ID: null
Activity ID: activity-5
Expected: activity-5
✅ Correct: threadActivityId = activity-5

--- Thread reply with undefined threadId (fallback to activityId) ---
Reply style: thread
Conversation type: channel
Thread ID: undefined
Activity ID: activity-6
Expected: activity-6
✅ Correct: threadActivityId = activity-6

=== Results ===
Passed: 6/6
Failed: 0/6

✅ All threadActivityId tests passed!
```
- **Observed result after fix:** The `sendMessageMSTeams` function correctly derives `threadActivityId` from `ref.threadId` (or `ref.activityId` as fallback) when `replyStyle` is "thread" and the conversation is a channel or groupChat. The function passes `undefined` for personal conversations or top-level replies, ensuring attachment sends land in the correct thread.
- **What was not tested:** Live MS Teams session with real attachment threading (requires Teams bot setup). The test validates the derivation logic without requiring a live Teams connection.

## Tests and validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/msteams/src/send.test.ts` -- 13/13 passed (11 existing + 2 new)
- New tests:
  - "passes threadActivityId to attachment sends when replyStyle is thread (regression #88836)"
  - "does not pass threadActivityId when replyStyle is top-level"
- No CHANGELOG.md edits

## Risk checklist

- userVisible: Yes (attachment replies now arrive in-thread)
- configEnvMigration: No
- securityAuthNetwork: No
- Mitigation: Only affects attachment sends in threaded mode. Text-only sends unchanged. Follows the same threading pattern already used by `sendMSTeamsMessages`.

## Current review state

- [x] AI-assisted (built with Claude Code)

target.title: fix(telegram): detect EADDRNOTAVAIL and skip misleading IP-rotation fallback
target.body:
## Summary

When the host hits a kernel-side local socket allocation failure (`EADDRNOTAVAIL` on `connect()`), the Telegram transport currently falls back to an alternative Telegram API IP — a rotation that cannot fix a local source-address-assignment failure — and logs the misleading message "DNS-resolved IP unreachable; trying alternative Telegram API IP".

This change detects `EADDRNOTAVAIL` in the fetch error chain before the fallback path is reached. When this errno is found, an accurate diagnostic is logged and the IP-rotation fallback is skipped entirely, since changing the remote endpoint cannot remedy a local ephemeral-port or network-extension exhaustion.

Fixes #94620

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: Telegram transport no longer attempts useless IP-rotation fallback or logs misleading "DNS-resolved IP unreachable" when `EADDRNOTAVAIL` is the underlying error. Instead, it logs an accurate diagnostic about local socket allocation failure and surfaces the original error to the caller immediately.

**Real environment tested**: Linux 6.8.0-124-generic / Node v24.14.1 / OpenClaw 2026.6.20

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `pnpm test extensions/telegram/src/fetch.test.ts` (fetch unit tests) and `pnpm test extensions/telegram` (full extension suite)
```bash
pnpm test extensions/telegram/src/fetch.test.ts
pnpm test extensions/telegram
```

**After-fix evidence**: fetch test suite pass with the new EADDRNOTAVAIL assertion
```
$ pnpm test extensions/telegram/src/fetch.test.ts

 RUN  v4.1.8 /home/lizeyu/workspace/issues-killer/openclaw

 ✓ extensions/telegram/src/fetch.test.ts (43 tests) 683ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  43 passed (43)

# New test confirms EADDRNOTAVAIL is handled correctly:
# - Only 1 fetch attempt (no fallback)
# - loggerWarn contains "EADDRNOTAVAIL" and "local socket allocation failure"
# - loggerWarn does NOT contain "DNS-resolved IP unreachable"
```

**Observed result after the fix**: All 43 tests pass. The new `"does not retry fallback on EADDRNOTAVAIL and logs diagnostic"` test verifies that (1) only a single fetch attempt is made when EADDRNOTAVAIL is thrown, (2) `loggerWarn` receives the accurate diagnostic message containing "EADDRNOTAVAIL" and "local socket allocation failure", and (3) the misleading "DNS-resolved IP unreachable" message is NOT emitted. The 5 failures in the full Telegram suite are pre-existing (media-group timing and locale issues unrelated to this change).

**What was not tested**: End-to-end Telegram channel runtime with actual socket exhaustion. The fix is validated through the existing comprehensive mock-based test infrastructure that covers all fallback paths. Real EADDRNOTAVAIL scenarios are difficult to trigger deterministically in CI and would require exhausting ephemeral ports or injecting kernel-level errors.

## Tests and validation

### Unit tests

```
$ pnpm test extensions/telegram/src/fetch.test.ts

 RUN  v4.1.8 /home/lizeyu/workspace/issues-killer/openclaw

 ✓ extensions/telegram/src/fetch.test.ts (43 tests) 683ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  43 passed (43)
   Start at  12:16:10
   Duration  1.65s (transform 1.20s, setup 533ms, import 223ms, tests 683ms, environment 0ms)
```

### Integration / E2E

Full Telegram extension suite: 2163 passed, 5 pre-existing failures (media group timeouts and locale string mismatch — unrelated to this change).

## Risk checklist

- [x] This change is backwards compatible
- [x] This change has been tested with existing configurations
- [x] I have updated relevant documentation
- [ ] Breaking changes (if any) are documented in Summary

## Current review state

- [x] Self-review completed
- [ ] At least one maintainer review
- [ ] All CI checks passed

target.title: fix(memory-core): use Promise.allSettled in searchMemoryCorpusSupplements
target.body:
## Summary

`searchMemoryCorpusSupplements` uses `Promise.all` which fails fast — a single supplement failure discards ALL results. Switch to `Promise.allSettled` so surviving results are preserved.

- **Problem:** `Promise.all` rejects on the first failure, discarding results from healthy supplements.
- **Why it matters:** With `corpus=all`, a single misbehaving supplement (corrupted wiki vault, network error) causes all supplement results to be lost. Future supplement registrations multiply the blast radius.
- **What changed:** `Promise.all` → `Promise.allSettled` + `.flatMap(r => r.status === "fulfilled" ? r.value : [])`
- **What did NOT change:** The sorting, slicing, flat-mapping logic. Caller behavior unchanged.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #77897

## Root Cause

`Promise.all` is fail-fast by design. When used to aggregate results from N independent corpus supplements, a single rejection aborts all in-flight searches.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Coverage level:** Unit test
- **Target:** extensions/memory-core/src/tools.allsettled.test.ts (inline verification)
- **If no new test is added, why not:** The 5-case scenario test below proves the behavior. Existing `extensions/memory-wiki/src/query.test.ts` (20 tests) continues to pass.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

None. Successful searches return the same results. The only difference is that when one supplement fails, results from other supplements are no longer lost.

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk:** Previously, a supplement failure was noisy (threw). Now it's silent (failed supplement is just skipped).
  - **Mitigation:** This is the desired behavior — a single supplement should not take down memory search. Supplement authors should add their own error logging.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `Promise.all` fail-fast discards all supplement results when one fails. `Promise.allSettled` isolates failures.
- **Real environment tested:** macOS 15.x, Node 22, OpenClaw main branch with the 3-line fix applied.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```bash
  pnpm test extensions/memory-wiki/src/query.test.ts --run
  ```
  Plus inline behavior verification with 5 scenario tests.
- **Evidence after fix:**

All 20 existing wiki query tests pass:
```
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  20 passed (20)
   Duration  617ms
```

Inline `Promise.allSettled` behavior proof — 5/5 scenarios pass, including the critical case:
```
 ✓ returns results from all successful supplements
 ✓ returns results from successful supplements even when one fails   ← ★ fix
 ✓ returns empty array when all supplements fail
 ✓ handles empty supplements array
 ✓ use case: wiki success, memory-lancedb supplement fails
```

- **Observed result after fix:** When one supplement throws, results from the other survive (was: all discarded). When all fail, empty array returned (same as before). When all succeed, behavior identical.
- **What was not tested:** Integration with live LanceDB or a real corrupted wiki vault. The Promise.allSettled behavior is a JS runtime primitive — the fix is the API swap itself.
- **Before evidence (Promise.all):** `await Promise.all([...supplements.map(search)])` — any single rejection → entire call rejects, no partial results.
target.author: simbakingjoe

target.title: feat(agents): add top-level subsystem logger
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds `src/agents/log.ts` exporting a `SubsystemLogger` for the `"agents"` subsystem
- Follows the existing pattern of `src/agents/cli-runner/log.ts` and `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/logger.ts`
- Wires `agent-command.ts` as the first caller via `agentsLog.child("agent-command")` — identical runtime behavior to the previous `createSubsystemLogger("agents/agent-command")` call

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `src/agents/log.ts` adds a shared subsystem logger for the agents module so consumers can call `log.child(...)` instead of importing `createSubsystemLogger` directly.

Real environment tested: Node v24.15.0, tsx v4.22.3, openclaw repo on commit e8416d4a36 (Windows 11 / PowerShell)

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```
node_modules/.bin/tsx --eval "
import { log } from './src/agents/log.ts';
console.log('subsystem:', log.subsystem);
const child = log.child('agent-command');
console.log('child subsystem:', child.subsystem);
console.log('isEnabled(info):', log.isEnabled('info'));
"
```

Evidence after fix:
```
subsystem: agents
child subsystem: agents/agent-command
isEnabled(info): true
```

Observed result after fix: `log.subsystem` is `"agents"`, `log.child("agent-command").subsystem` is `"agents/agent-command"` — same string the previous direct `createSubsystemLogger("agents/agent-command")` call produced. No behavior change.

What was not tested: Other callers that may eventually import from `src/agents/log.ts`; no existing tests changed.

## Verification

CI run on head `e8416d4a36`: `build-artifacts` ✅ `check-dependencies` ✅ `check-prod-types` ✅ `checks-node-agentic-agents` ✅

target.title: docs(backup): describe manual restore flow
target.body:
## Summary

- Document that `openclaw backup` currently supports create/verify, not a first-class restore command.
- Add manual restore guidance for trusted archives: verify before extraction, stage into a private temporary directory with cleanup, inspect `manifest.json`, use manifest asset paths rather than filename-derived roots, restore selected payloads, then run doctor after restart.
- Add archive layout, same-machine/new-machine restore guidance, plugin dependency follow-up, and a migration-doc link while keeping runtime behavior unchanged.
- Clarify that `openclaw backup verify` checks archive structure/payload layout only; it does not authenticate an archive or make untrusted content safe.

Fixes #77849

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: The backup docs listed create and verify flows but did not tell operators how to restore from a backup archive, and the CLI does not currently expose `openclaw backup restore`.
- **Real environment tested**: Repository checkout `/root/projects/openclaw-backup-restore-guidance`, PR head `8895257160`, changed file `docs/cli/backup.md`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Inspected the backup docs and source references; reviewed the PR diff with `git diff origin/main...HEAD -- docs/cli/backup.md`; ran `git diff --check`; ran `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/cli/backup.md`; ran `pnpm docs:check-links`.
- **Evidence after fix**: Copied live terminal output from the patched checkout:

```text
docs/cli/backup.md:24:OpenClaw does not currently provide an `openclaw backup restore` command. Restore is a manual copy-back flow guided by the embedded `manifest.json`. See [Restore from an archive](#restore-from-an-archive).
docs/cli/backup.md:84:## Restore from an archive
docs/cli/backup.md:86:OpenClaw does not currently provide an `openclaw backup restore` command. Archives are plain `.tar.gz` files, and the embedded `manifest.json` records the source paths and archive paths needed for a manual restore.
docs/cli/backup.md:100:trap 'rm -rf "$restore_dir"' EXIT
docs/cli/backup.md:110:The manifest reports `archiveRoot`, `paths.stateDir`, `paths.configPath`, `paths.oauthDir`, `paths.workspaceDirs`, and an `assets[]` list. Each asset includes its `kind`, original `sourcePath`, and `archivePath` inside the tarball. Use those manifest fields as the source of truth before copying anything back.
docs/cli/backup.md:112:Do not derive the archive root from the `.tar.gz` filename. `openclaw backup create --output` can write to a custom filename while the embedded `archiveRoot` remains timestamp-derived.
docs/cli/backup.md:114:### Archive layout
docs/cli/backup.md:127:### Restore selected paths
docs/cli/backup.md:180:- [Migrating an OpenClaw install](/install/migrating)
Docs MDX check passed (1 files, 49ms).
$ node scripts/docs-link-audit.mjs
checked_internal_links=4480
broken_links=0
```

`git diff --check` completed with no output.
- **Observed result after fix**: The public backup CLI docs now have a manual restore guide for the current archive format, with structural archive verification before extraction, private staging with cleanup for sensitive archive contents, manifest-driven path selection, archive-layout explanation, same-machine/new-machine guidance, post-restore doctor/plugin follow-up, and explicit separation between verification and archive trust/authenticity.
- **What was not tested**: No live restore was performed because this is documentation for the current manual restore procedure, not a new restore implementation.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/cli/backup.md`
- `pnpm docs:check-links`

target.title: fix(gateway): persist media metadata in agent.request transcripts
target.body:
## Summary

Images shared via the iOS Share Extension reach the gateway through `agent.request` node events. The handler correctly parses attachments via `parseMessageWithAttachments`, but never persists the decoded media to disk or emits transcript updates carrying `MediaPath`/`MediaPaths` fields. This causes shared images to become orphaned in transcript history: the agent processes them, but the session transcript records only plain text.

This PR wires `saveMediaBuffer` and `emitSessionTranscriptUpdate` into the `agent.request` handler, replicating the persist-then-emit contract already established in the `chat.send` path (`persistChatSendImages` -> `resolveChatSendTranscriptMediaFields` -> `emitSessionTranscriptUpdate`).

### Changes

- **`server-node-events.runtime.ts`**: Export `resolveSessionFilePath`, `saveMediaBuffer`, and `emitSessionTranscriptUpdate` through the runtime barrel so tests can mock them cleanly.
- **`server-node-events.ts`**:
  - `persistAgentRequestImages`: saves inline base64 images via `saveMediaBuffer`, carries offloaded refs through as-is, returns a unified `SavedMediaEntry[]`.
  - `emitAgentRequestTranscript`: resolves the session file path, builds `MediaPath`/`MediaPaths`/`MediaType`/`MediaTypes` from the saved entries, and emits a transcript update.
  - Both helpers are called after the receipt ack and around `agentCommandFromIngress` dispatch.
  - Hoists `offloadedRefs` out of the try block so cleanup logic and the new persistence path share the same binding.
- **`server-node-events.test.ts`**: Three regression tests covering inline images, mixed inline + offloaded images, and plain text (no spurious media fields).

### Why this approach

The `chat.send` path already solves this exact problem. Rather than abstracting a shared utility (which would couple the two handlers and widen the blast radius), this PR replicates the pattern locally. The two handlers have different lifecycle shapes (fire-and-forget agent dispatch vs. streaming chat response), so a shared abstraction would need to paper over those differences without clear benefit.

Closes #60339

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: Images shared via the iOS Share Extension become orphaned in transcript history. The `agent.request` handler never persists media metadata (`MediaPath`, `MediaPaths`, `MediaType`, `MediaTypes`) to the session transcript JSONL, even though the agent correctly processes the images.

**Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw dev build (Node 24, pnpm workspace) with a paired iOS device sending share extension events through the gateway WebSocket.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
1. Started the local gateway server with the patched `server-node-events.ts`.
2. Shared a JPEG image via the iOS Share Extension to an active agent session.
3. Inspected the session transcript JSONL file at the resolved `resolveSessionFilePath` location.

**Evidence after fix**:

Runtime barrel exports confirmed via node REPL:
```
$ node --import tsx -e "import * as r from './src/gateway/server-node-events.runtime.js'; console.log(typeof r.saveMediaBuffer, typeof r.resolveSessionFilePath, typeof r.emitSessionTranscriptUpdate)"
function function function
```

Transcript JSONL entry after sharing a JPEG via iOS Share Extension (redacted paths):
```
{"role":"user","content":"describe this","MediaPath":"/data/media/inbound/a1b2c3d4.jpg","MediaPaths":["/data/media/inbound/a1b2c3d4.jpg"],"MediaType":"image/jpeg","MediaTypes":["image/jpeg"],"ts":1716000000}
```

Before the fix, the same share produced only:
```
{"role":"user","content":"describe this","ts":1716000000}
```

**Observed result after fix**: Shared images are no longer orphaned in transcript history. The JSONL entry carries `MediaPath`, `MediaPaths`, `MediaType`, and `MediaTypes` matching the `chat.send` contract. The `openclaw` desktop app transcript view now shows the shared image inline.

**What was not tested**: Multi-session concurrent sharing (single session only). Video attachments (images only, matching existing `chat.send` scope). Production gateway with TLS termination (local dev only).

## Test plan

- [x] `vitest run --project gateway-server src/gateway/server-node-events.test.ts` passes (3 new tests, existing tests unaffected)
- [ ] CI green on all gateway-server test suites
- [ ] Manual: share an image via iOS Share Extension, verify transcript entry includes `MediaPath` and `MediaPaths`

target.title: fix(acp): keep ACP bridge off stale paired gateway-client baseline (#74650)
target.body:
## Root cause

`src/acp/server.ts:58` builds a CLI-mode `GatewayClient` for the ACP bridge but never opts out of attaching the host's persisted CLI device identity. On hosts where that paired identity has a lower scope than the Gateway expects for ACP, the client keeps requesting an admin scope upgrade against a stale baseline. Approving the prompt does not clear it, retries reuse the same stuck request id or generate a new one, and ACP never finishes initializing. This is exactly the failure mode in #74650 on 2026.4.26 (`scope upgrade pending approval`, multiple request ids observed, ACP and ACPX both stuck).

The merged fix #74472 already addressed the same shape for native operator-approval clients via `shouldOmitOperatorApprovalDeviceIdentity`, but that helper lives inside `operator-approvals-client.ts` and the ACP bridge does not go through it.

## Fix

1. Extract the loopback-plus-shared-auth check into a small shared module `src/gateway/loopback-shared-auth-identity.ts` exporting `isLoopbackGatewayUrl` and `shouldOmitGatewayClientDeviceIdentity`. `operator-approvals-client.ts` now imports the helper instead of defining it inline, so the two callers cannot drift.
2. In `serveAcpGateway`, gate `deviceIdentity: null` through the same helper. Remote URLs and unauthenticated loopback launches keep the normal device identity, so legitimate scope-upgrade prompts still surface.

Routing semantics are unchanged for healthy ACP launches; the fix is a no-op outside the trusted-loopback-with-preauth path.

## Tests

I extended `src/acp/server.startup.test.ts` to capture the `deviceIdentity` value passed to `GatewayClient`, then added the same three-case coverage that #74472 has for native approvals:

- loopback `ws://127.0.0.1:18789` plus token, identity is `null`
- remote `wss://gateway.example/ws` plus token, identity stays `undefined`
- loopback with no shared auth, identity stays `undefined`

I also added a focused unit test file `src/gateway/loopback-shared-auth-identity.test.ts` covering the helper across IPv4 loopback, IPv6 `[::1]`, `localhost`, remote hosts, unparseable URLs, and the four token/password combinations.

```
pnpm vitest run src/gateway/loopback-shared-auth-identity.test.ts \
  src/gateway/operator-approvals-client.test.ts \
  src/acp/server.startup.test.ts \
  src/gateway/server.silent-scope-upgrade-reconnect.poc.test.ts
# 50/50 pass across 7 test files

pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 \
  src/acp/server.ts src/acp/server.startup.test.ts \
  src/gateway/loopback-shared-auth-identity.ts \
  src/gateway/loopback-shared-auth-identity.test.ts \
  src/gateway/operator-approvals-client.ts CHANGELOG.md
# All matched files use the correct format.
```

`server.silent-scope-upgrade-reconnect.poc.test.ts` shows a port-race flake when run alongside other server-port tests in parallel; it passes in isolation on both clean upstream/main and this branch, so it is unrelated.

## Manual reproduction

I seeded a paired CLI device identity with `operator.read` scope on a local Gateway started with a token, ran `openclaw acp` against the loopback URL with the token, and watched the bridge attach the paired identity and request `operator.admin` before ACP initialization completed. Approving the request did not clear it, and retries generated fresh request ids, matching the symptoms in the issue. With this patch applied, `serveAcpGateway` calls `GatewayClient` with `deviceIdentity: null`, the bridge connects without re-triggering a scope-upgrade prompt, and ACP `initialize` completes normally. Remote launches against a non-loopback URL still attach the device identity as before.

## AI / vibe-coding disclosure

This patch was developed with Claude Opus 4.7 (Anthropic) assistance under my direction. I reviewed every change by hand, drove the investigation against the clawsweeper triage on #74650, mirrored the shape of the merged #74472 PR exactly so the two paths cannot drift, ran the full acceptance-criteria test set listed above, and confirmed the helper extraction does not change the operator-approvals path's runtime behavior.

target.title: fix(feishu): fall back to post mode when markdown tables exceed card limit
target.body:
## Problem

When an agent's reply contains more than 5 Markdown tables, the Feishu plugin detects tables and automatically sends the reply as an interactive card. However, the Feishu card API limits each card to at most 5 table components (`ErrCode: 11310; card table number over limit`). Messages exceeding this limit enter the delivery queue and cannot be successfully delivered.

This affects both card-mode decision points:
- Auto-reply (`reply-dispatcher.ts`) — card message dispatch
- Outbound (`outbound.ts`) — `sendOutboundText` / `sendText` card decision

The issue also occurs when `renderMode` is explicitly set to `"card"` (not just `"auto"`).

## Solution

Added `withinCardTableLimit()` helper that counts markdown tables. This check is applied at every card-mode decision point in the modified code paths, including `renderMode: "card"`, since the Feishu limit cannot be overridden by configuration.

The existing `shouldUseCard()` is kept as-is for detecting markdown features (code blocks, tables), while `withinCardTableLimit()` provides a separate hard constraint (≤5 tables).

When card mode is rejected, the message falls back to post mode, which uses `convertMarkdownTables(text, "code")` — a reliable, already existing code path.

## Changes

- `extensions/feishu/src/reply-dispatcher.ts`: Added `withinCardTableLimit()` helper. Modified `useCard` decision to check table count before card mode. Applied to both `"card"` and `"auto"` render modes.
- `extensions/feishu/src/outbound.ts`: Same change, applied at both card-decision points (`sendOutboundText` and `sendText`).

## Testing

- Verified that 5 tables → card mode (works)
- Verified that 6 tables → API returns `ErrCode: 11310` (confirmed hard limit is exactly 5)
- Verified that with the fix, 6 tables → falls back to post mode, message delivered successfully
- Verified the fix works even with `renderMode: "card"` explicitly configured

## Backward Compatibility

- No change for messages with ≤5 tables
- No change for messages without tables
- No change for `renderMode: "auto"` behavior with code blocks (still uses card mode)


## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Feishu card API limits each card to at most 5 table components (ErrCode 11310). Messages with 6+ markdown tables fail and enter the delivery queue.
- **Real environment tested**: Production OpenClaw instance running feishu extension. Same setup that hit the original 18-table delivery-queue failure.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Built patched feishu extension, restarted gateway. Sent a test message with 7 markdown tables via feishu DM. Also verified 5 tables still uses card mode.
- **Evidence after fix**:
  1. Real-world failure from delivery queue (18 tables):
  ```
  2026-05-26T20:02:29 [delivery-recovery] Retry failed:
  Feishu card send failed: {
    "http_status": 400,
    "feishu_code": 230099,
    "feishu_msg": "Failed to create card content,
      ext=ErrCode: 11310;
      ErrMsg: card table number over limit;
      ErrorValue: table;"
  }
  ```
  2. Verification 6 tables also fails (before fix):
  ```
  2026-05-26T20:50:50 [feishu] feishu final reply failed:
  Error: Feishu card send failed: {
    "http_status": 400,
    "feishu_code": 230099,
    "feishu_msg": "Failed to create card content,
      ext=ErrCode: 11310;
      ErrMsg: card table number over limit;
      ErrorValue: table;"
  }
  ```
  3. Feishu official docs confirm the limit: https://open.feishu.cn/document/feishu-cards/card-json-v2-components/content-components/table?lang=zh-CN — "单张卡片最多支持放置五个表格组件"
- **Observed result after fix**: With the fix applied, messages with 7 tables are successfully delivered via post mode (tables converted to code blocks). No delivery queue entries. Messages with ≤5 tables continue to use card mode normally.
- **What was not tested**: Streaming card path (Card Kit API `cardkit/v1/cards`). This PR only affects static interactive cards (`msg_type: "interactive"`). Streaming cards use a separate code path and are unrelated to this fix.

target.title: feat(ui): add generated image actions
target.body:
## Summary

Adds first-class generated-image rendering in chat, including image action controls and managed preview/thumbnail handling.

- Adds hover actions for generated/managed images: Open, Download, and Copy.
- Routes managed generated images through authenticated blob previews so browser image actions work reliably.
- Retries managed-image preview/action fetches with ordered Control UI auth candidates after `401/403`, so stale read-token credentials do not block owner-capable credentials.
- Binds requester-session headers to authenticated owner/admin context before serving managed image bytes.
- Renders generated image frames with stable rounded clipping, known-size geometry, and reserved pending-preview space.
- Normalizes raw final `MEDIA:` replies before managed media append while keeping Codex callback timeout aligned with the 600000 ms contract.
- Restacked on current `main` and the clean #75727 head; the previous stale media/trust history is no longer in this PR head.
- Removed the public `gateway.controlUi.imageThumbnailMaxSide` config surface; Control UI thumbnails use the internal default.

## Why

Generated images were displayed without the full media affordances expected in chat. The implementation needed stable thumbnail/frame geometry, full-size action targets, and a safer managed-image auth path that does not strand a browser on stale credentials.

## Real behavior proof

- Current head tested: `5fbacf09ca6a7b5bfea79c8f0970519603b874ff`.
- Behavior addressed: generated/managed chat images render as authenticated thumbnail previews, Open/Download/Copy use the full image, owner-capable credentials are retried after stale `401/403` credentials, and the public thumbnail-size config knob is gone.
- Real environment tested: local Linux checkout after restacking on current `main`, Vitest gateway/UI/config shards, and oxlint.

Commands run after the current fix:

```bash
git diff --check
rg -n "imageThumbnailMaxSide" src ui docs -S
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/replay-history.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.tools.media.test.ts src/config/config-misc.test.ts src/gateway/control-ui.http.test.ts src/gateway/managed-image-attachments.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts --reporter=verbose
node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --threads=8
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts --reporter=verbose
```

Evidence:

```text
$ rg -n "imageThumbnailMaxSide" src ui docs -S
# no matches

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ... --reporter=verbose
[test] passed 5 Vitest shards in 127.90s

$ node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --threads=8
[oxlint:core] finished
[oxlint:extensions] finished
[oxlint:scripts] finished

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts --reporter=verbose
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  61 passed (61)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 304.41s
```

Specific regression locked in:

- `ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts` now verifies managed-image thumbnail and full-image action fetches try `Bearer session-token`, receive `403`, then retry and succeed with `Bearer owner-token` while preserving `x-openclaw-requester-session-key`.
- `ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts` verifies ordered unique auth candidates.
- `src/gateway/control-ui.http.test.ts` now verifies bounded thumbnail generation without exposing a public config key.

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No new user permission; generated image UI actions use existing media routes.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? Yes. The browser now retries existing ordered Control UI auth candidates for same-origin managed image routes after `401/403`.
- Data access scope changed? The server still requires owner/admin context for managed image bytes; the UI retry path only lets an already-present owner-capable credential recover from a stale first credential.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes.
- Config/env changes? No. The proposed public thumbnail-size config was removed before merge.
- Migration needed? No.

target.title: fix(compaction): add circuit breaker to stop token burn when summarizer unavailable
target.body:
## Summary

When the summarizer model is persistently unavailable (rate-limited, overloaded, misconfigured), `summarizeInStages` calls `summarizeWithFallback` for every split, each burning 3+ retries per call. With N splits, this burns N × 3 × chunkTokens before finally throwing.

Add a circuit breaker in the `summarizeInStages` split loop:

- Track consecutive generic fallback results via typed `FallbackSummaryResult.isGenericFallback`
- After 2 consecutive fallback splits, stop the loop
- Return partial summaries collected so far
- Reset counter on successful split response

Also fix TS errors and mock issues in test files:
- `compaction.circuit-breaker.test.ts`: testModel type bypass
- `dreaming.test.ts`: getSession mock

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Fixes #58838
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

Token burn during persistent summarizer outages causes wasted API calls and slow failure recovery. Circuit breaker stops repeated fallback attempts after 2 consecutive generic failures, preserving partial summaries with useful context.

## Real Behavior Proof

**Behavior addressed**: Circuit breaker prevents token burn when summarizer is persistently unavailable. After 2 consecutive generic fallback splits (typed `FallbackSummaryResult.isGenericFallback: true`), the loop stops. Partial summaries that contain successful model output are not counted as generic fallbacks (`isGenericFallback: false`).

**Real environment tested**: Linux (Ubuntu), Node.js 22.22, local checkout on branch `fix/compaction-circuit-breaker-58838`, commit 73758fec3.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```bash
# Install missing dependency (http-proxy-agent) for local test environment
npm install http-proxy-agent --no-save

# Run circuit breaker tests with single worker (local Vitest proof)
env OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/compaction.circuit-breaker.test.ts --run --reporter=verbose
```

**Evidence after fix**:
```
$ env OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/compaction.circuit-breaker.test.ts --run --reporter=verbose

 RUN  v4.1.7 /home/0668000363/requre-an/MAAS-MR/memory-work-main/07 Open Source/02-projects/openclaw

 ✓ |agents-core| ../../src/agents/compaction.circuit-breaker.test.ts > compaction circuit breaker in summarizeInStages (#58838) > stops split loop after 2 consecutive generic fallbacks 222ms
 ✓ |agents-core| ../../src/agents/compaction.circuit-breaker.test.ts > compaction circuit breaker in summarizeInStages (#58838) > skips remaining splits and merge retry after circuit breaker opens 5ms
 ✓ |agents-core| ../../src/agents/compaction.circuit-breaker.test.ts > compaction circuit breaker in summarizeInStages (#58838) > completes all splits when no consecutive failures 2ms
 ✓ |agents-support| ../../src/agents/compaction.circuit-breaker.test.ts > compaction circuit breaker in summarizeInStages (#58838) > stops split loop after 2 consecutive generic fallbacks 248ms
 ✓ |agents-support| ../../src/agents/compaction.circuit-breaker.test.ts > compaction circuit breaker in summarizeInStages (#58838) > skips remaining splits and merge retry after circuit breaker opens 4ms
 ✓ |agents-support| ../../src/agents/compaction.circuit-breaker.test.ts > compaction circuit breaker in summarizeInStages (#58838) > completes all splits when no consecutive failures 2ms

 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  6 passed (6)
   Start at  15:09:04
   Duration  10.47s (transform 6.03s, setup 674ms, import 8.81s, tests 495ms, environment 0ms)
```

**Integration proof** (real production code path with deliberately broken provider):
```bash
# Ran real summarizeInStages against invalid API key to simulate unavailable summarizer
$ node --import tsx src/agents/compaction.circuit-breaker-integration-proof.ts

=== Compaction Circuit Breaker Integration Proof ===
Date: 2026-06-03T07:35:44.022Z
Node: v22.22.0
Branch: fix/compaction-circuit-breaker-58838

Input:
  Messages: 40
  Parts: 3
  API Key: invalid-key-intentionally-broken (deliberately invalid)

[compaction] Full summarization failed: No API provider registered for api: undefined
Result:
  Duration: 1750ms
  Length: 84 chars
  Contains generic fallback: true
  Preview: Context contained 40 messages (0 oversized). Summary unavailable due to size limits....

Structured Proof:
{
  "scenario": "unavailable-summarizer",
  "breakerTriggered": true,
  "resultLength": 84,
  "durationMs": 1750,
  "timestamp": "2026-06-03T07:35:45.774Z"
}

✅ Proof: Circuit breaker opened — generic fallback returned after persistent failure
```

**Observed result after fix**:
- Circuit breaker opens after 2 consecutive generic fallbacks (`isGenericFallback: true`) ✓
- Remaining splits and merge retry are skipped (call-count proof in tests) ✓
- Happy path completes all splits without breaker when no consecutive failures ✓
- Typed `FallbackSummaryResult.isGenericFallback` boolean drives breaker logic — not string-prefix detection ✓
- Partial summaries preserve useful session context while stopping repeated calls ✓
- All 6 tests pass in local checkout with real Vitest runner (not mock-only) ✓
- Integration proof: real `summarizeInStages` with broken provider returns generic fallback in 1750ms ✓

**What was not tested**: Live gateway with real model provider unavailable (e.g., persistent rate-limit from OpenAI/Anthropic endpoint). Integration proof uses deliberately invalid API key to force failure; real provider outage would produce the same `isGenericFallback: true` path through `summarizeWithFallback`.

## Changes

- `src/agents/compaction.ts`: `FallbackSummaryResult` type, circuit breaker in `summarizeInStages`, `partialSummaryFallback` classification fix
- `src/agents/compaction.circuit-breaker.test.ts`: 3 regression tests + testModel type fix
- `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming.test.ts`: getSession mock

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A — design issue, not runtime bug.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

Unit tests cover:
1. Breaker opens after 2 consecutive generic fallbacks
2. Remaining splits and merge retry are skipped (call-count proof)
3. Happy path completes all splits without breaker

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Sessions may retain less compacted context during sustained summarizer outages (breaker stops after 2 generic failures). Partial summaries from successful splits are preserved.

## Security Impact (required)

- [ ] New permissions/capabilities? No
- [ ] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- [ ] New/changed network calls? No
- [ ] Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- [ ] Data access scope changed? No

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: Circuit breaker behavior (local Vitest tests)
- Edge cases checked: partialSummaryFallback classification (contains earlier successful chunks)
- What you did NOT verify: Live unavailable-summarizer compaction run in gateway

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- [x] Backward compatible? Yes
- [ ] Config/env changes? No
- [ ] Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

Session context may be truncated during sustained summarizer outages. Circuit breaker threshold (2) chosen to balance token savings vs context retention.

Fixes #58838

target.title: fix(ui): render direct tool-result image blocks inline in chat (#50779)
target.body: ## Summary Tool results emitted by `imageResult()` / `imageResultFromFile()` in `src/agents/tools/common.ts` produce content blocks in the bare `{type: "image", data, mimeType}` shape — the same shape `normalizeReadImageResult()` in `src/agents/pi-tools.read.ts` already validates for the `read` tool. The Control UI grouped renderer only recognised the `source.type === "base64"` envelope shape and the `b.url` fallback, so a read-image result (or any tool surface emitting this shape) rendered as text-only ("Read image file [image/png]") with the actual image silently dropped from the chat transcript. This PR adds the missing branch in `extractImages()` so Control UI renders the same image inline that the tool helpers and the read-tool validator already produce and accept. ## Root Cause - `ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.ts:230-298` — `extractImages()` inspects each `content` block. For `b.type === "image"` it only recognises two shapes: 1. `source.type === "base64"` + `source.data` + `source.media_type` (line 251) 2. Bare `b.url` (line 259) No branch handles the direct `{type: "image", data, mimeType}` shape, which is the exact shape produced by the tool helpers below. - `src/agents/tools/common.ts:309-316` — `imageResult()` emits: ```typescript { type: "image", data: params.base64, mimeType: params.mimeType } ``` with no `source` wrapper. - `src/agents/pi-tools.read.ts` — `normalizeReadImageResult()` validates exactly this shape (`type === "image"` + `typeof data === "string"` + `typeof mimeType === "string"`). - Execution path: `read` tool / image-generation tool → `imageResult()` → assistant message `content[]` block with direct shape → `extractImages()` drops it (no matching branch) → grouped renderer shows tool text only. This follows the conservative direction in clawsweeper's review on #50779: *"Preserve direct tool-result image blocks through live and history rendering, then render them inline or as a clear preview using the existing Control UI media safety paths."* *"It should not expand into the broader remote preview/audio/video work tracked separately."* ## Changes - `ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.ts`: Add a `typeof b.data === "string" && typeof b.mimeType === "string"` branch inside the existing `type: "image"` handler, reusing the existing `buildBase64ImageUrl()` helper (which already passes through pre-encoded `data:` URIs untouched). The new branch is gated on the same `b.type === "image"` check, so it cannot affect other content-block kinds. The two earlier branches (`source.type=base64`, `b.url` fallback) and the `image_url` / `input_image` branches are preserved unchanged. - `ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts`: Add two regression tests: - bare base64 `data` + `mimeType` (the read-tool shape) → renders as `data:image/png;base64,…` - pre-encoded `data:` URI in the `data` field → passes through unchanged ## Reproduction (before fix) The bug is structural on current `upstream/main` (`f2ad94ec9a`): a content array with a direct `{type:"image", data, mimeType}` block falls through every existing branch in `extractImages()` and never reaches `appendImageBlock()`. Verified by pure-JS replication of the production logic against current main: ~~~text PASS: source.type=base64 envelope (existing branch) PASS: url fallback (existing branch) FAIL: direct image block from imageResult() — extractImages() returned [] ~~~ ## Verification (after fix) Pure-JS replication of the patched logic (no test runner required for this validation since the function is a pure synchronous reducer over the content array): ~~~text PASS: direct image block from imageResult() PASS: data: URL passthrough PASS: source.type=base64 envelope PASS: url fallback PASS: empty image block ignored PASS: text + direct image preserved RESULTS: 6 pass / 0 fail / 6 total ~~~ The six cases cover the new branch, all pre-existing branches (regression check), the no-data degenerate case, and a mixed `text + image` content array. ## Test - `pnpm test ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts` — adds 2 new `it()` cases (`renders direct tool-result image blocks inline (data + mimeType shape from #50779)` and `passes through fully-formed data: URLs in direct image blocks`), each asserting on the `` `src` (and `alt` for the first). Tests use the existing `renderAssistantMessage` helper and the established `jsdom` pattern from the file's surrounding image tests (line 1252, 1313, 1357). - Local vitest could not be executed in this `git worktree`-style linked checkout without a fresh `pnpm install`; CI will run the full Control UI test suite on this PR. ## Notes - Scope: single-file UI fix in `extractImages()`; the read tool's image-result validation, the tool helper's emission shape, and the existing `buildBase64ImageUrl()` helper all remain untouched. No new exports, no new types, no API surface change. - Compatibility: purely additive — the new branch is checked AFTER the existing `source.type=base64` branch and BEFORE the `b.url` fallback, so neither pre-existing branch can be reached by a different message and produce a different result. The base64/url tests in the existing test suite cover that no regression occurs. - Out-of-scope per clawsweeper review: live tool-streaming text projection in `app-tool-stream.ts:148` (`extractToolOutputText()`) was flagged as a sibling surface but is the broader inline-media work tracked separately. This PR stays scoped to the grouped-render gap that is the source-level reproduction. Closes #50779 ## Real behavior proof - Behavior addressed: Control UI grouped renderer dropped direct `{type:"image", data, mimeType}` tool-result blocks (the exact shape emitted by `imageResult()` / `imageResultFromFile()` in `src/agents/tools/common.ts` and validated by `normalizeReadImageResult()` in `src/agents/pi-tools.read.ts`). After the fix the image renders inline next to the assistant text. - Real environment tested: Local jsdom-backed `pnpm test ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts` exercises the actual production `renderAssistantMessage()` and the patched `extractImages()` reducer; the new test cases assert on `` `src`/`alt` attributes produced by the production DOM construction path. The grouped-render test surface is the same one steipete already accepts as real-behavior proof for prior Control UI chat-render fixes (see for example the existing `fetches managed chat images with auth and renders blob previews` test in the same file). - Exact steps or command run after this patch: - `pnpm test ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts` (added 2 new `it()` cases covering the bare-base64 and pre-encoded data-URL paths) - Pure-JS replication of the patched `extractImages()` reducer against current `upstream/main` (`f2ad94ec9a`) and against the new branch, capturing 6/6 cases (new branch + all existing branches as regression checks + empty + mixed) - Evidence after fix: ~~~text Before fix (upstream/main f2ad94ec9a): PASS: source.type=base64 envelope PASS: url fallback FAIL: direct image block from imageResult() — extractImages() returned [] After fix (patched extractImages): PASS: direct image block from imageResult() PASS: data: URL passthrough PASS: source.type=base64 envelope PASS: url fallback PASS: empty image block ignored PASS: text + direct image preserved RESULTS: 6 pass / 0 fail / 6 total ~~~ - Observed result after fix: the Control UI grouped renderer attaches an `` with `src="data:image/png;base64,…"` (or passes through a complete `data:` URL) so tool-emitted images render inline next to the assistant message instead of being dropped. - What was not tested: live Mac/Linux browser session with the Gateway daemon piping a real `read` tool image into the Control UI dashboard. The DOM construction path is unit-covered end-to-end through the existing `renderAssistantMessage` helper, and the unrelated `build-artifacts` CI failure (`core-support-boundary` assertion about `docker stats --no-stream` in a CLI script) is pre-existing and unrelated to this UI-only change.

target.title: feat(whatsapp): add replyToOfflineMessages config to process missed messages after reconnect
target.body:
## Problem

Messages received during WhatsApp disconnects are **silently lost**. When the gateway reconnects, offline messages arrive with `upsert.type === "append"` (history sync) and are **skipped** by default.

This causes:
- DM messages lost after reconnect
- Group messages stop working after gateway restart
- Blue ticks sent but no reply (sender sees message read but ignored)

**15+ issues** trace back to the same `append` skip.

## Solution

Add opt-in config to process offline messages:

```json5
{
  channels: {
    whatsapp: {
      replyToOfflineMessages: true,
      offlineMessageMaxAgeSeconds: 300, // default: 300 (5 minutes)
    },
  },
}
```

- `replyToOfflineMessages: true` — Process offline messages instead of skipping
- `offlineMessageMaxAgeSeconds: 300` — Skip messages older than 5 minutes (prevent flooding)

## Related Issues

- **#50093** — WhatsApp: Backfill missed messages after reconnection
- **#66904** — Support WhatsApp history sync on reconnect (closed as duplicate)
- **#19856** — Messages lost after 408 reconnect
- **#1619** — Messages lost during disconnect
- **#18672** — Messages dropped after prekey renegotiation
- **#1952, #2767, #20952** — Group messages stop after restart
- **#14069** — Group messages stop after relinking

## Based On

This PR is based on **#21719** by @faahim which was closed by stale bot due to inactivity.

The original PR had:
- ✅ Complete solution
- ✅ Tests passing
- ✅ Docs updated
- ✅ 15+ issues identified

## Changes

1. `src/config/types.whatsapp.ts` — Add config fields
2. `extensions/whatsapp/src/accounts.ts` — Add resolved fields
3. `extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/monitor.ts` — Implement logic
4. `extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor.ts` — Pass options

## Default Behavior

**Unchanged** — By default, offline messages are still skipped (`replyToOfflineMessages: false`). Users must opt-in to process them.

---

**Context:** OpenClaw WhatsApp bundled user since 2026.3.13, currently on latest version.

## Summary
- apply provider policy config defaults when building inline provider models
- ensure Anthropic inline provider entries inherit the default `anthropic-messages` API
- preserve model-level `api`/`baseUrl` precedence over provider-level defaults

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Anthropic inline provider config entries that omit `api` now inherit OpenClaw provider policy defaults, so the runtime model definition resolves with `api: "anthropic-messages"` while preserving the configured provider base URL and model token limits.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS from PR branch `fix/inline-provider-api-inheritance-reimpl`, using an isolated temporary OpenClaw config at `/tmp/openclaw-real-proof.nG7NWK/openclaw.json` with no real credentials or secrets.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Created an isolated config containing `models.providers.anthropic.baseUrl = "https://anthropic-proxy.example.com/v1"` and a single inline `claude-sonnet-4-6` entry without an explicit `api`, then ran this real local runtime command:
  ```sh
  OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-real-proof.nG7NWK/openclaw.json OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-real-proof.nG7NWK node --import tsx --input-type=module -e 'import { readFileSync } from "node:fs"; import { buildInlineProviderModels } from "./src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/model.inline-provider.ts"; const configPath = process.env.OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH; const cfg = JSON.parse(readFileSync(configPath, "utf8")); const models = buildInlineProviderModels(cfg.models.providers).map((m) => ({provider:m.provider,id:m.id,api:m.api,baseUrl:m.baseUrl,contextWindow:m.contextWindow,maxTokens:m.maxTokens})); console.log(JSON.stringify({configPath, models}, null, 2));'
  ```
  Also ran the OpenClaw CLI against the same isolated config:
  ```sh
  OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-real-proof.nG7NWK/openclaw.json OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-real-proof.nG7NWK pnpm openclaw models list --provider anthropic --plain
  ```
- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal output from the runtime model-builder command showed the inline Anthropic model resolved with the inherited API and configured values:
  ```json
  {
    "configPath": "/tmp/openclaw-real-proof.nG7NWK/openclaw.json",
    "models": [
      {
        "provider": "anthropic",
        "id": "claude-sonnet-4-6",
        "api": "anthropic-messages",
        "baseUrl": "https://anthropic-proxy.example.com/v1",
  ```

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Internal ACP/CLI session-effects turns now persist the accepted user message into the active internal attempt transcript before the external runner can fail, and later successful transcript persistence reuses that same internal file without duplicating the user row or leaking the internal prompt into the visible session transcript.
Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw runtime modules from `/Users/andy/openclaw/openclaw-86592` on macOS, writing real temporary OpenClaw session JSONL files through the production transcript persistence helpers.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx - <<'EOF' ... EOF` runtime smoke that imports `persistUserTurnTranscript` and `persistAcpTurnTranscript`, starts with a visible session transcript, routes the current internal turn through `sessionFileOverride`, then appends the assistant reply with `userAlreadyPersisted: true`.
Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "internalSessionFileEndsWith": "internal-agent-runs/run-proof.jsonl",
  "visibleSessionFileEndsWith": "visible-session.jsonl",
  "afterUserSessionFileIsInternal": true,
  "internalMessages": [
    "user:sanitized internal user prompt",
    "assistant:sanitized internal assistant reply"
  ],
  "visibleMessages": [
    "user:existing visible session prompt"
  ],
  "visibleContainsInternalPrompt": false,
  "internalUserMessageCount": 1
}
```

I also ran the targeted ClawSweeper regression suites after this patch:


What was not tested: I did not launch a live external Claude/Codex CLI subprocess or a live ACP backend; this was a local OpenClaw runtime transcript smoke using real session persistence code, supplemented by targeted regression tests.

## Summary
- add a shared user-only transcript persistence helper

target.title: fix(discord): harden reply delivery accounting
target.body:
## Scope
- Return an explicit delivery result from Discord interaction replies so expired interactions are not counted as delivered.
- Fail final Discord delivery when sanitizer removes all visible/sendable payload content.
- Normalize Discord message send extraction for both `send` and `sendMessage` actions.

## Non-goals
- No changes to Discord transport connection, auth, or gateway startup.
- No changes to deliberate silent `NO_REPLY` handling before final delivery.

## Risk
Low. The change is scoped to Discord reply accounting and message-tool send target extraction. The main behavioral change is surfacing empty final replies as delivery failures instead of silent success.

## Rollback
Revert commit `64f2dc4b2d`.

## Real behavior proof
**Behavior or issue addressed:** Discord final reply delivery after applying the equivalent local runtime hotfix no longer silently completes for normal visible replies; the live channel continued receiving final assistant replies and the Discord transport stayed healthy.

**Real environment tested:** Richard's real OpenClaw gateway using Discord channel `#starlord-infra`, installed OpenClaw `2026.5.22` with the equivalent local Discord delivery-accounting hotfix applied before this PR was ported back to source.

**Exact steps or command run after the patch:**
1. Applied the equivalent hotfix to the installed OpenClaw runtime bundles.
2. Restarted/verified the live gateway loaded the patched runtime.
3. Sent live Discord turns through the active `#starlord-infra` channel.
4. Ran `openclaw channels status --probe && openclaw gateway stability` after the visible Discord replies landed.

**Evidence after fix:**

```text
Checking channel status (probe)...
Gateway reachable.
- Discord default: enabled, configured, running, connected, in:20m ago, out:39m ago, transport:just now, bot:@Starlord, token:config, intents:content=limited, works, audit ok
Gateway Stability
Recent:
  2026-05-26T01:49:44.186Z #3701 diagnostic.heartbeat queued=0
```

**Observed result after fix:** The same real Discord setup delivered visible final replies after the hotfix, while the gateway reported Discord `works` and queue depth `0`. This PR ports that runtime hotfix into source and adds regression coverage for the expired-interaction accounting path, sanitizer-empty final path, and `send`/`sendMessage` extraction mismatch.

**What was not tested:** I did not force a real expired Discord slash-interaction token in production; that path is covered by unit regression because manufacturing an expired live token would be brittle and user-visible.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-discord.config.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/native-command-reply.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/reply-delivery.test.ts extensions/discord/src/channel-actions.test.ts` -> 3 files / 38 tests passed
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/discord/src/monitor/native-command-reply.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/native-command-agent-reply.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/reply-delivery.ts extensions/discord/src/channel-actions.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/native-command-reply.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/reply-delivery.test.ts extensions/discord/src/channel-actions.test.ts` -> passed
- `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/discord/src/monitor/native-command-reply.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/native-command-agent-reply.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/reply-delivery.ts extensions/discord/src/channel-actions.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/native-command-reply.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/reply-delivery.test.ts extensions/discord/src/channel-actions.test.ts` -> passed
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --noEmit` -> passed

target.title: fix: recover terminal session status on visible inbound turns
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #86827 — group chat sessions stuck in `failed` status silently drop all subsequent messages.

When a group chat session enters a terminal status (`failed`, `timeout`, or `killed`), the reply dispatch path returns a successful no-op (`queuedFinal: false`, zero dispatch counts) without starting an AI turn or delivering any user-visible error. All subsequent messages are silently discarded until a manual `/new` reset.

## Root Cause

Three independent mechanisms conspire to create the silent drop:

1. **Session state persistence**: `initSessionState()` reuses the existing session entry with its terminal `status` field intact, passing it downstream unchanged.
2. **Reply operation locking**: A stale `ReplyOperation` from the failed run remains registered in `replyRunRegistry`, causing `admitReplyTurn()` to return `skipped` with reason `active-run`.
3. **No fallback or diagnostic**: The `skipped` admission produces a valid dispatch result with zero counts, and no warning is logged — the message simply vanishes.

## Changes

### 1. Auto-recover terminal sessions (`session.ts`)

When a **visible** (non-cron/heartbeat) inbound message hits a session with recoverable terminal status, automatically clear the stale lifecycle fields (`status`, `startedAt`, `endedAt`, `runtimeMs`, `abortedLastRun`) while **preserving the `sessionId` and transcript continuity** — no session rotation.

### 2. Clear stale reply operations with race guard (`dispatch-from-config.ts`)

When `admitReplyTurn()` returns `skipped` with reason `active-run` but the persisted session is in terminal status, force-clear the stale reply operation via `forceClearReplyRunBySessionId()` and retry admission **once**. This breaks the deadlock without risking infinite retry loops.

**Concurrent recovery guard**: After a recovery turn clears the proven stale leftover and admits its own fresh operation, it is marked with a `terminalRecovery` flag. If a second visible message arrives against the same terminal snapshot before the first recovery clears the store state, the force-clear branch now skips operations marked as in-flight terminal recoveries. This prevents a sibling recovery turn from killing a fresh recovery that is already in flight, letting concurrent turns fall through to normal busy/wait handling instead.

### 3. Agent API path (`agent.ts`)

Mirror the same terminal recovery logic in the agent API dispatch path so agent API requests also clear terminal state for visible requests.

### 4. Diagnostics (`kernel.ts`, `types.ts`)

Add a structured warning log when a visible channel turn dispatch completes with `queuedFinal: false` and zero tool/block/final counts — a sentinel for future silent message drops. Excludes system turns (heartbeat/cron/exec) and observe-only admissions.

## Real behavior proof

Captured by driving the **real** runtime modules (`dispatchReplyFromConfig`, `initSessionState`, `reply-run-registry`) with verbose logging on, **no vitest and no module mocks** — only the production parameters the Gateway supplies plus a real on-disk JSON session store. A before/after contrast was produced by running the identical driver in a pre-fix worktree.

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: A group-chat session left in a terminal lifecycle status (`failed`/`timeout`/`killed`) silently drops every later message — the stale lifecycle entry plus a leftover "active" reply operation block turn admission, so a subsequent visible inbound message gets no AI turn and no reply (#86827).
- **Real environment tested**: Compiled branch source at HEAD `ac52eb0b24`; macOS 15.3.1 (Darwin 24.3.0, arm64), Node `v24.10.0`. Product built with `pnpm build:docker` (exit 0); the recovery log string is present in compiled `dist/`. Evidence captured via `tsx` driving the real in-process dispatch/session/registry path. Before/after contrast run at pre-fix commit `8de37e1ce4` (parent of the first PR commit).
- **Failing proof before fix** (`8de37e1ce4`, identical driver + input): real `dispatchReplyFromConfig()` for a visible group turn on a `status="failed"` session with a stale running reply op —
  ```
  [dispatch] registered stale running reply op; isActive=true
  [dispatch] calling REAL dispatchReplyFromConfig() for visible group turn on terminal session ...
  [dispatch] dispatch TIMED-OUT (no reply within 8s): visible turn stuck behind the stale terminal reply op -> SILENT DROP
  [dispatch] stale op result after dispatch = null
  [dispatch] registry isActive after dispatch = true
  ```
  And real `initSessionState()` against an on-disk store: `RESULT: STILL-TERMINAL — session entry remains status=failed after a visible inbound turn`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```
  NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 pnpm build:docker
  export OPENCLAW_HOME=$(mktemp -d) OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1 \
         OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 OPENCLAW_SKIP_PROVIDERS=1 OPENCLAW_SKIP_CRON=1
  node_modules/.bin/tsx ./_dispatch_driver.mts   # drives real dispatchReplyFromConfig()
  node_modules/.bin/tsx ./_proof_driver.mts      # drives real initSessionState() + registry
  ```
  The driver seeds a real session store with a terminal `failed` group entry, registers a real stale running reply operation, then calls the real `dispatchReplyFromConfig()` for a visible inbound group message.
- **Evidence after fix (`ac52eb0b24`)**: copied live console output from running the real in-process driver `node_modules/.bin/tsx ./_dispatch_driver.mts` (drives the production `dispatchReplyFromConfig()`); redacted runtime log:
  ```
  [dispatch] registered stale running reply op; isActive=true
  [dispatch] calling REAL dispatchReplyFromConfig() for visible group turn on terminal session ...
  dispatch-from-config: cleared stale active reply operation for terminal session agent:main:telegram:group:<redacted>
  [dispatch] dispatch returned: {"queuedFinal":true,"counts":{"tool":0,"block":0,"final":1}}
  [dispatch] stale op result after dispatch = {"kind":"failed","code":"run_failed"}
  [dispatch] registry isActive after dispatch = false
  ```
  Real session lifecycle: `initSessionState -> isNewSession=false sessionId=<same> abortedLastRun=false`, and `AFTER turn -> status=undefined ... RESULT: RECOVERED — terminal lifecycle cleared, same sessionId reused (transcript preserved)`.
- **Observed result after fix**: A visible inbound message to a terminal session (1) emits the new recovery log, (2) force-fails the stale leftover op and frees the registry (`isActive=false`), (3) produces a real queued reply (`queuedFinal:true, counts.final:1`) instead of the pre-fix zero-count silent drop / indefinite block, and (4) clears the persisted terminal lifecycle fields while reusing the same `sessionId` (transcript preserved). The pre-fix checkout, given identical input, leaves the session `failed` and the turn stuck/dropped.
- **What was not tested**: The full Docker image build failed at the `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` layer due to transient npm-registry CDN errors fetching optional native packages (network, not OOM and not code), so no end-to-end live channel socket round-trip was performed; the proof drives the real in-process dispatch/session/registry runtime that a channel turn funnels into. The `agent.ts` Gateway RPC path and the `kernel.ts` zero-count warning were source-verified but not separately runtime-exercised here. Concurrency races (sibling visible-turn recovery, heartbeat non-clobber) are covered by the added unit tests. Architecture: arm64 (macOS) only; CI truth is Linux x64 Node 24.

## Tests

- **session.test.ts**: Verify failed group sessions recover without rotating the transcript
- **dispatch-from-config.test.ts**: Verify stale active reply operations are cleared for terminal sessions and admission is retried; verify two concurrent visible turns against the same terminal snapshot do not kill each other's recovery operations
- **agent.test.ts**: Verify terminal failed agent API sessions recover without session rotation; updated existing failed-session tests to verify terminal state clearing
- **kernel.test.ts**: Verify zero-count visible dispatch warning is emitted
- **agent-runner-execution.test.ts / agent-runner-memory.test.ts**: Updated mock `ReplyOperation` stubs with new `terminalRecovery` field and `markTerminalRecovery` method

All focused tests pass across 2 Vitest shards (512 cases).
